nodes.label: AI transcription accuracy for technical terms
nodes.grounding_span: the transcription issue is far better - I think that is due to the relative lack of subjectivity required

nodes.label: AI as implementation executor
nodes.grounding_span: I would normally say 'here's what I need to happen...' so more independently than ongoing collaborations

nodes.label: AI accuracy in numerical tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI unreliability and lack of context

nodes.label: AI as proactive team member
nodes.grounding_span: at the moment my workplace use of AI is entirely responsive to my requests rather than it proactively producing stuff

nodes.label: AI adoption in workplace
nodes.grounding_span: My workplace culture is quite risk averse... whereas I have embraced it more wholeheartedly

nodes.label: AI for low-stakes tasks
nodes.grounding_span: depending on the importance or impact of the task is where I would want to have control over it

nodes.label: Trust and reliability in AI outputs
nodes.grounding_span: build that confidence would be a strong start

nodes.label: Personal competence and skill development
nodes.grounding_span: frees me up to develop or hone my skills in other areas

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for transcription
nodes.grounding_span: the transcription issue is far better

nodes.label: Frustration with AI for complex synthesis
nodes.grounding_span: More frustration. Particularly when trying to compose a progress update paper

nodes.label: Positive view of team AI practices for continuity
nodes.grounding_span: if AI could start to do some of that it'd be great

nodes.label: Pragmatic acceptance of AI as a tool
nodes.grounding_span: I would always be willing to at least try AI initially for everything

nodes.label: Contextual detail calibration — adjusts input detail based on situation complexity
nodes.grounding_span: depending on the importance or impact of the task is where I would want to have control over it

nodes.label: New team member analogy for AI onboarding
nodes.grounding_span: treating AI like you would a new team member with that learning and check-in process
